junie.name: Junie Okafor
junie.intro: Red-eyed, hugging a paper coffee cup. “You're the one who knew her voice, aren't you. From the books.”

dialogue.line: She taught me everything. She'd run my pickups at midnight just so I'd sound less scared. I keep waiting for her to walk in and fix my breathing.

dialogue.topic: Did you notice anything strange about the new books?
dialogue.line: Can I tell you something stupid? The breaths. In the new books. Nobody breathes that evenly reading aloud. I thought I was grieving wrong. But you hear it too, don't you.
Recording pickups, like always — I'm here at odd hours. My keycard's all over the logs. I know how that looks. I didn't do anything.

theo.name: Theo Marsh
theo.intro: Composed. Too composed. “She's finally resting. She'd been so tired.”

dialogue.line: Her heart, they think. She'd worked herself to the bone finishing the series. She wouldn't stop.

dialogue.topic: When did you last hear from her?
dialogue.line: We talked all the time. She sent me notes just last week — little voice memos, you know how she was.

dialogue.topic: Tell me about the new recordings.
dialogue.line: We finished what she started. It's what she wanted. Don't make it ugly.

priya.name: Priya Anand
priya.intro: She ends a call and turns, smooth as glass. “I'm handling the estate. Everything's in order.”

dialogue.topic: What's at stake with this release?
dialogue.line: The biggest launch of her career. Posthumous, now, I suppose. The numbers will be… enormous.
Standard terms. I really can't discuss the estate. Della knew exactly what she signed.

gareth.name: Gareth Vale (via assistant)
gareth.intro: He's abroad; his assistant speaks for him, bright and rehearsed.

dialogue.line: [Assistant] Mr. Vale is at the Frankfurt book fair. He said the world should hear her one last time. The event's in thirty-six hours — he insisted.

dialogue.topic: Did Aldercrest doubt the new recordings?
dialogue.line: [Assistant] I — I really couldn't say. There was an internal note. Something about whether the dailies 'sounded like her.' I shouldn't have said that.
